events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: commercial electrical wire pulling feeders & branch, staged, observed condition: no lubricant on long pull. Need choose correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice wrong. Reference NEC 200.6, 210.5 identification. But lubricant relates to wire pulling, not identification. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Observed no lubricant on long pull. Work staged, not begun. Is this fail? The task: choose correct tool, material, or replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. So observed choice wrong = no lubricant. Correct approach: use NEC-approved pulling lubricant compatible with cable insulation/jacket, e.g., polymer or wax-based cable pulling compound, for long pulls to reduce friction, prevent insulation damage, meet manufacturer and NEC 110.3(B) installation instructions. References maybe NEC 110.3(B) installation and use, 200.6/210.5 identification not directly relevant but on hand. Could mention 110.3(B). Decision fail because non-compliant / defective staged condition? Since staged and no lubricant planned, maybe fail if inspection would reject. Risk medium/high? Long pull without lubricant can damage conductors, insulation, increase tension beyond max allowable (NEC 110.3(B), manufacturer max pulling tension). Could cause conductor damage, rework, safety. Risk high? Since work not started, hazard potential but not immediate energized danger. But non-compliant installation method. I'd set risk high due to potential insulation damage and code violation. component_conditions: installed-defective? It's staged, not installed. But category may apply: installed-defective? The scenario says component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. Since no lubricant is missing material/method, not component condition. Could list installed-defective? Hmm. The observed field condition is no lubricant on long pull. The work is staged. Component condition categories that may apply: installed-defective. Maybe because the pull setup is defective as staged. Use ["installed-defective"] maybe. percent_complete 0 (staged). value 0. confidence 0.9. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "", order [], workable [] unless asked. findings: long feeder/branch circuit pull lacks pulling lubricant; omission can exceed allowable pulling tension and abrade/damage conductor insulation. actions: apply code-listed, cable-manufacturer-approved pulling lubricant to conduit/raceway and cable jacket before pull; verify pull tension and use appropriate pulling equipment; inspect cables after pull for insulation damage. rationale: NEC 110.3(B) requires equipment used per listing/installation instructions; long pulls require lubricant to limit friction and tension and protect insulation. NEC 200.6/210.5 identification references on hand but not central. Need include references. Maybe ["NEC 110.3(B)", "NEC 200.6", "NEC 210.5"]. The prompt says apply reference material rather than quoting mechanically. Include them.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside. Write to /app/answer.json. In final, just JSON.

events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "staged",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 0,

  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Long feeder/branch circuit pull is staged without pulling lubricant.",
    "Omission of lubricant on a long pull can cause excessive friction, exceed allowable pulling tension, and damage conductor insulation or jacket."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Apply a code-listed, cable-manufacturer-approved pulling lubricant compatible with the conductor insulation and raceway before starting the pull.",
    "Verify maximum allowable pulling tension and use proper pulling equipment and techniques.",
    "Inspect conductors after the pull for insulation damage and replace any damaged cables."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.3(B) requires electrical equipment to be installed and used in accordance with its listing and manufacturer instructions. Long wire pulls require lubricant to limit friction and tension, preventing insulation damage and ensuring a code-compliant installation. The staged setup without lubricant is therefore wrong and must be corrected before pulling begins.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC 200.6",
    "NEC 210.5"
  ]
}
